(Italian for Professional Football Italian League), commonly known as **Lega Pro**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (Pro League), is the league that runs the third tier of professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") competitions in Italy, the [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C"); the third division of Italian football. It used several names in the past, including **Lega Professionisti Serie C**. It is a full member of [European Leagues](/wiki/European_Leagues "European Leagues").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

by the [FIGC](/wiki/FIGC "FIGC") Commissioner [Bruno Zauli](/wiki/Bruno_Zauli "Bruno Zauli") on the base of the old Lega IV Serie. The League managed three [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") and six [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") groups with 162 clubs.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The league was intermediate between professionals and amateurs. Former C clubs protested for their lost [Lega Calcio](/wiki/Lega_Calcio "Lega Calcio") membership, and they spent the following twenty years to regain a professional status. The league, led by [Artemio Franchi](/wiki/Artemio_Franchi "Artemio Franchi"), was expanded in 1967 to 222 clubs. A new reform in 1978 abolished the Serie D and divided the C into two fully professional championships, the [Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1") and the [Serie C2](/wiki/Serie_C2 "Serie C2"), with 108 members in six groups. The new name was **Lega Nazionale Serie C**, and its status became professional. After 2000 the Serie C2 was reduced and later abolished in 2014, to give way to a [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") in three groups.

The  **Lega Italiano Calcio Professionistico** (Italian for Professional Football Italian League), commonly known as **Lega Pro**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (Pro League), is the league that runs the third tier of professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") competitions in Italy, the [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C"); the third division of Italian football. It used several names in the past, including **Lega Professionisti Serie C**. It is a full member of [European Leagues](/wiki/European_Leagues "European Leagues").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lega_Pro&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The league was created in 1959 as **Lega Nazionale Semiprofessionisti** by the [FIGC](/wiki/FIGC "FIGC") Commissioner [Bruno Zauli](/wiki/Bruno_Zauli "Bruno Zauli") on the base of the old Lega IV Serie. The League managed three [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") and six [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") groups with 162 clubs.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The league was intermediate between professionals and amateurs. Former C clubs protested for their lost [Lega Calcio](/wiki/Lega_Calcio "Lega Calcio") membership, and they spent the following twenty years to regain a professional status. The league, led by [Artemio Franchi](/wiki/Artemio_Franchi "Artemio Franchi"), was expanded in 1967 to 222 clubs. A new reform in 1978 abolished the Serie D and divided the C into two fully professional championships, the [Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1") and the [Serie C2](/wiki/Serie_C2 "Serie C2"), with 108 members in six groups. The new name was **Lega Nazionale Serie C**, and its status became professional. After 2000 the Serie C2 was reduced and later abolished in 2014, to give way to a [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") in three groups.
